Africa.name: Algeria
Africa.funFact: Algeria is the largest country in Africa by area.

Africa.name: Angola
Africa.funFact: Angola is one of the world's largest oil producers in sub-Saharan Africa.

Africa.name: Benin
Africa.funFact: Benin was the birthplace of the Vodun (Voodoo) religion.

Africa.name: Botswana
Africa.funFact: Botswana is home to the Okavango Delta, the world's largest inland delta.

Africa.name: Burkina Faso
Africa.funFact: Burkina Faso means 'Land of Honest Men' in the local Mooré and Dioula languages.

Africa.name: Burundi
Africa.funFact: Burundi is one of the smallest countries in Africa and among the most densely populated.

Africa.name: Cabo Verde
Africa.funFact: Cabo Verde is a volcanic archipelago of 10 islands off the west coast of Africa.

Africa.name: Cameroon
Africa.funFact: Cameroon is often called 'Africa in miniature' for its geological and cultural diversity.

Africa.name: Central African Republic
Africa.funFact: The Central African Republic has one of the world's least light-polluted night skies.

Africa.name: Chad
Africa.funFact: Lake Chad was once one of the largest lakes in Africa but has shrunk by 90% since the 1960s.

Africa.name: Comoros
Africa.funFact: Comoros is known as the 'Perfume Islands' for its production of ylang-ylang and vanilla.

Africa.name: DR Congo
Africa.funFact: The Congo River is the deepest river in the world, reaching over 220 metres.

Africa.name: Republic of Congo
Africa.funFact: Brazzaville and Kinshasa are the closest pair of capital cities in the world, separated by the Congo River.

Africa.name: Côte d'Ivoire
Africa.funFact: Côte d'Ivoire produces about 40% of the world's cocoa supply.

Africa.name: Djibouti
Africa.funFact: Lake Assal in Djibouti is the lowest point in Africa at 155m below sea level.

Africa.name: Egypt
Africa.funFact: The Great Pyramid of Giza was the tallest man-made structure for over 3,800 years.

Africa.name: Equatorial Guinea
Africa.funFact: Equatorial Guinea is the only country in Africa with Spanish as an official language.

Africa.name: Eritrea
Africa.funFact: Asmara is a UNESCO World Heritage Site known for its Modernist architecture.

Africa.name: Eswatini
Africa.funFact: Eswatini (formerly Swaziland) is one of the last remaining absolute monarchies in the world.

Africa.name: Ethiopia
Africa.funFact: Ethiopia uses a unique calendar that is 7-8 years behind the Gregorian calendar.

Africa.name: Gabon
Africa.funFact: About 85% of Gabon's land area is covered by rainforest.

Africa.name: Gambia
Africa.funFact: The Gambia is the smallest country on mainland Africa, shaped like a narrow strip along the Gambia River.

Africa.name: Ghana
Africa.funFact: Ghana was the first sub-Saharan African country to gain independence from colonial rule in 1957.

Africa.name: Guinea
Africa.funFact: Guinea has the world's largest reserves of bauxite, the ore used to make aluminium.

Africa.name: Guinea-Bissau
Africa.funFact: Guinea-Bissau's Bijagós Archipelago is a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve with rare saltwater hippos.

Africa.name: Kenya
Africa.funFact: Kenya is named after Mount Kenya, the second-highest mountain in Africa.

Africa.name: Lesotho
Africa.funFact: Lesotho is the only country in the world that lies entirely above 1,000 metres elevation.

Africa.name: Liberia
Africa.funFact: Liberia was founded by freed American slaves and its capital is named after US President James Monroe.

Africa.name: Libya
Africa.funFact: Libya is about 90% desert and contains part of the Sahara.

Africa.name: Madagascar
Africa.funFact: About 90% of Madagascar's wildlife is found nowhere else on Earth.

Africa.name: Malawi
Africa.funFact: Lake Malawi contains more species of fish than any other lake in the world.

Africa.name: Mali
Africa.funFact: Timbuktu in Mali was a major center of Islamic learning and trade in the 15th century.

Africa.name: Mauritania
Africa.funFact: The Eye of the Sahara (Richat Structure) in Mauritania is visible from space.

Africa.name: Mauritius
Africa.funFact: Mauritius was the only known habitat of the now-extinct Dodo bird.

Africa.name: Morocco
Africa.funFact: The University of al-Qarawiyyin in Fez, Morocco, is the oldest existing university in the world, founded in 859 AD.

Africa.name: Mozambique
Africa.funFact: Mozambique's flag features an AK-47 rifle, making it the only national flag with a modern firearm.

Africa.name: Namibia
Africa.funFact: The Namib Desert is considered the oldest desert in the world, at about 55 million years old.

Africa.name: Niger
Africa.funFact: Niger is home to the last herd of wild West African giraffes.

Africa.name: Nigeria
Africa.funFact: Nigeria is the most populous country in Africa with over 220 million people.

Africa.name: Rwanda
Africa.funFact: Rwanda has the highest proportion of women in parliament of any country in the world.

Africa.name: São Tomé and Príncipe
Africa.funFact: São Tomé and Príncipe is the smallest country in Africa and the second-smallest African nation by population.

Africa.name: Senegal
Africa.funFact: The Dakar Rally, one of the most famous off-road races, was originally run from Paris to Dakar.

Africa.name: Seychelles
Africa.funFact: Seychelles is the smallest country in Africa by population and has the Coco de Mer, the world's largest seed.

Africa.name: Sierra Leone
Africa.funFact: Freetown was founded as a home for freed African-American and Caribbean slaves.

Africa.name: Somalia
Africa.funFact: Somalia has the longest coastline on mainland Africa, stretching over 3,000 km.

Africa.name: South Africa
Africa.funFact: South Africa has three capital cities: Pretoria (executive), Cape Town (legislative), and Bloemfontein (judicial).

Africa.name: South Sudan
Africa.funFact: South Sudan became the world's newest country when it gained independence in 2011.

Africa.name: Sudan
Africa.funFact: Sudan has more ancient pyramids than Egypt, with over 200 in the Meroë region.

Africa.name: Tanzania
Africa.funFact: Mount Kilimanjaro in Tanzania is the highest free-standing mountain in the world.

Africa.name: Togo
Africa.funFact: Togo is one of the narrowest countries in the world, only 115 km wide at its widest.

Africa.name: Tunisia
Africa.funFact: Tunisia contains the ruins of Carthage, the ancient rival of Rome.

Africa.name: Uganda
Africa.funFact: Uganda is home to more than half of the world's remaining mountain gorilla population.

Africa.name: Zambia
Africa.funFact: Victoria Falls on the Zambia-Zimbabwe border is the world's largest sheet of falling water.

Africa.name: Zimbabwe
Africa.funFact: The Great Zimbabwe ruins are the largest ancient stone structure south of the Sahara.

Asia.name: Afghanistan
Asia.funFact: Afghanistan is home to lapis lazuli mines that have been active for over 6,000 years.

Asia.name: Armenia
Asia.funFact: Armenia was the first country to adopt Christianity as a state religion in 301 AD.

Asia.name: Azerbaijan
Asia.funFact: Baku's Flame Towers are shaped like flames, honouring Azerbaijan's nickname 'Land of Fire'.

Asia.name: Bahrain
Asia.funFact: Bahrain was home to the ancient Dilmun civilization and is one of the earliest centers of pearl diving.

Asia.name: Bangladesh
Asia.funFact: Bangladesh has the world's largest river delta, the Ganges-Brahmaputra Delta.

Asia.name: Bhutan
Asia.funFact: Bhutan measures national success by Gross National Happiness instead of GDP.

Asia.name: Brunei
Asia.funFact: The Sultan of Brunei's palace, Istana Nurul Iman, is the world's largest residential palace with 1,788 rooms.

Asia.name: Cambodia
Asia.funFact: Angkor Wat in Cambodia is the largest religious monument in the world.

Asia.name: China
Asia.funFact: The Great Wall of China stretches over 21,000 km and took over 2,000 years to build.

Asia.name: Cyprus
Asia.funFact: Nicosia is the world's last divided capital city.

Asia.name: Georgia
Asia.funFact: Georgia is considered one of the birthplaces of wine, with 8,000-year-old wine residues found in clay jars.

Asia.name: India
Asia.funFact: India has the most post offices in the world, with over 155,000 locations.

Asia.name: Indonesia
Asia.funFact: Indonesia has the largest number of active volcanoes in the world, with about 130.

Asia.name: Iran
Asia.funFact: Iran is home to one of the world's oldest civilizations, with settlements dating to 7000 BC.

Asia.name: Iraq
Asia.funFact: Ancient Mesopotamia (modern Iraq) is known as the 'Cradle of Civilization' where writing was invented.

Asia.name: Israel
Asia.funFact: The Dead Sea, bordering Israel, is the lowest point on Earth's surface at 430m below sea level.

Asia.name: Japan
Asia.funFact: Japan has more than 6,800 islands and over 100 active volcanoes.

Asia.name: Jordan
Asia.funFact: Petra, Jordan's 'Rose City', was carved directly into red sandstone cliffs over 2,000 years ago.

Asia.name: Kazakhstan
Asia.funFact: Kazakhstan is the largest landlocked country in the world and the 9th largest country overall.

Asia.name: Kuwait
Asia.funFact: Kuwait has about 6% of the world's proven oil reserves.

Asia.name: Kyrgyzstan
Asia.funFact: Over 80% of Kyrgyzstan is covered by mountains, earning it the nickname 'Switzerland of Central Asia'.

Asia.name: Laos
Asia.funFact: Laos is the most heavily bombed country per capita in history, due to the Vietnam War era.

Asia.name: Lebanon
Asia.funFact: The Cedars of Lebanon are among the most famous trees in history and appear on the national flag.

Asia.name: Malaysia
Asia.funFact: The Petronas Twin Towers in Kuala Lumpur were the tallest buildings in the world from 1998 to 2004.

Asia.name: Maldives
Asia.funFact: The Maldives is the lowest-lying country in the world, with an average ground level of just 1.5 metres.

Asia.name: Mongolia
Asia.funFact: Mongolia is the least densely populated country in the world, with about 2 people per sq km.

Asia.name: Myanmar
Asia.funFact: Myanmar's Shwedagon Pagoda in Yangon is covered with hundreds of gold plates and topped with thousands of diamonds.

Asia.name: Nepal
Asia.funFact: Nepal's flag is the only non-rectangular national flag in the world.

Asia.name: North Korea
Asia.funFact: North Korea uses its own calendar, called the Juche calendar, starting from 1912.

Asia.name: Oman
Asia.funFact: Oman's frankincense trees have been harvested for trade for over 5,000 years.

Asia.name: Pakistan
Asia.funFact: K2, the second-highest mountain in the world, is located in Pakistan's Karakoram range.

Asia.name: Palestine
Asia.funFact: The city of Jericho in Palestine is considered one of the oldest continuously inhabited cities in the world.

Asia.name: Philippines
Asia.funFact: The Philippines is made up of over 7,600 islands and has one of the longest coastlines in the world.

Asia.name: Qatar
Asia.funFact: Qatar has one of the highest GDPs per capita in the world thanks to its natural gas reserves.

Asia.name: Saudi Arabia
Asia.funFact: Saudi Arabia's Rub' al Khali (Empty Quarter) is the largest contiguous sand desert in the world.

Asia.name: Singapore
Asia.funFact: Singapore is both a city and a country, and one of only three city-states in the world.

Asia.name: South Korea
Asia.funFact: South Korea has the fastest average internet speed in the world.

Asia.name: Sri Lanka
Asia.funFact: Sri Lanka is one of the world's top tea-producing countries and was formerly known as Ceylon.

Asia.name: Syria
Asia.funFact: Damascus is one of the oldest continuously inhabited cities in the world, dating back over 11,000 years.

Asia.name: Taiwan
Asia.funFact: Taiwan produces over 60% of the world's advanced semiconductor chips.

Asia.name: Tajikistan
Asia.funFact: Over 90% of Tajikistan is mountainous, making it Central Asia's most mountainous country.

Asia.name: Thailand
Asia.funFact: Thailand is the only Southeast Asian country never colonized by a European power.

Asia.name: Timor-Leste
Asia.funFact: Timor-Leste is one of the youngest nations, gaining independence in 2002.

Asia.name: Turkey
Asia.funFact: Istanbul is the only city in the world that spans two continents, Europe and Asia.

Asia.name: Turkmenistan
Asia.funFact: The Darvaza Gas Crater in Turkmenistan, known as the 'Door to Hell', has been burning since 1971.

Asia.name: United Arab Emirates
Asia.funFact: The Burj Khalifa in Dubai is the tallest building in the world at 828 metres.

Asia.name: Uzbekistan
Asia.funFact: Uzbekistan is one of only two doubly landlocked countries in the world.

Asia.name: Vietnam
Asia.funFact: Sơn Đoòng Cave in Vietnam is the world's largest known cave passage by volume.

Asia.name: Yemen
Asia.funFact: The old city of Sana'a has been inhabited for over 2,500 years and features distinctive tower houses.

Europe.name: Albania
Europe.funFact: Albania has over 750,000 bunkers built during the communist era — roughly one for every four citizens at the time.

Europe.name: Andorra
Europe.funFact: Andorra la Vella is the highest capital city in Europe at 1,023 metres.

Europe.name: Austria
Europe.funFact: Vienna has been ranked the most liveable city in the world multiple times.

Europe.name: Belarus
Europe.funFact: Belarus is home to the Białowieża Forest, one of the last primeval forests in Europe.

Europe.name: Belgium
Europe.funFact: Belgium has over 1,000 varieties of beer and produces 220,000 tonnes of chocolate per year.

Europe.name: Bosnia and Herzegovina
Europe.funFact: Sarajevo hosted the 1984 Winter Olympics and is known as the 'Jerusalem of Europe' for its religious diversity.

Europe.name: Bulgaria
Europe.funFact: Bulgaria is one of the oldest countries in Europe, established in 681 AD.

Europe.name: Croatia
Europe.funFact: The necktie originated in Croatia — Croatian soldiers wore distinctive knotted scarves in the 17th century.

Europe.name: Czechia
Europe.funFact: Czechia has the highest beer consumption per capita in the world.

Europe.name: Denmark
Europe.funFact: Denmark's Tivoli Gardens, opened in 1843, is one of the oldest amusement parks in the world.

Europe.name: Estonia
Europe.funFact: Estonia was the first country to offer online voting in national elections in 2005.

Europe.name: Finland
Europe.funFact: Finland has about 3 million saunas for 5.5 million people — roughly one sauna per household.

Europe.name: France
Europe.funFact: France is the most visited country in the world, receiving over 89 million tourists annually.

Europe.name: Germany
Europe.funFact: Germany has over 1,500 different varieties of beer and hosts the famous Oktoberfest.

Europe.name: Greece
Europe.funFact: Greece has more archaeological museums than any other country in the world.

Europe.name: Hungary
Europe.funFact: Budapest has the largest thermal water cave system in the world.

Europe.name: Iceland
Europe.funFact: Iceland has no army and is heated almost entirely by geothermal energy.

Europe.name: Ireland
Europe.funFact: Ireland's Cliffs of Moher rise 214 metres above the Atlantic Ocean.

Europe.name: Italy
Europe.funFact: Italy has more UNESCO World Heritage Sites than any other country in the world.

Europe.name: Kosovo
Europe.funFact: Kosovo declared independence in 2008, making it one of the youngest countries in Europe.

Europe.name: Latvia
Europe.funFact: Riga's Art Nouveau district has the highest concentration of Art Nouveau buildings in the world.

Europe.name: Liechtenstein
Europe.funFact: Liechtenstein is one of only two doubly landlocked countries in the world, along with Uzbekistan.

Europe.name: Lithuania
Europe.funFact: Lithuania was the last country in Europe to be Christianized, converting in the 14th century.

Europe.name: Luxembourg
Europe.funFact: Luxembourg has the highest GDP per capita in the world.

Europe.name: Malta
Europe.funFact: Valletta is the smallest national capital in the EU by area and is a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

Europe.name: Moldova
Europe.funFact: Moldova has one of the world's largest wine cellars, Mileștii Mici, with over 200 km of underground tunnels.

Europe.name: Monaco
Europe.funFact: Monaco is the most densely populated country in the world and hosts the famous Monaco Grand Prix.

Europe.name: Montenegro
Europe.funFact: Montenegro's Bay of Kotor is often called the southernmost fjord in Europe.

Europe.name: Netherlands
Europe.funFact: About a third of the Netherlands lies below sea level, protected by an extensive system of dikes and dams.

Europe.name: North Macedonia
Europe.funFact: Lake Ohrid in North Macedonia is one of the oldest and deepest lakes in Europe.

Europe.name: Norway
Europe.funFact: Norway's coastline, including fjords and islands, stretches over 100,000 km.

Europe.name: Poland
Europe.funFact: Poland's Wieliczka Salt Mine has been operating continuously since the 13th century.

Europe.name: Portugal
Europe.funFact: Portugal is the oldest country in Europe with the same borders since 1139.

Europe.name: Romania
Europe.funFact: Romania's Palace of the Parliament is the heaviest building in the world and the second-largest administrative building after the Pentagon.

Europe.name: Russia
Europe.funFact: Russia spans 11 time zones and is the largest country in the world by area.

Europe.name: San Marino
Europe.funFact: San Marino claims to be the oldest republic in the world, founded in 301 AD.

Europe.name: Serbia
Europe.funFact: Belgrade is one of the oldest cities in Europe, with settlements dating back 7,000 years.

Europe.name: Slovakia
Europe.funFact: Slovakia has more castles and châteaux per capita than any other country in the world.

Europe.name: Slovenia
Europe.funFact: Over half of Slovenia is covered by forest, making it one of the greenest countries in Europe.

Europe.name: Spain
Europe.funFact: Spain's La Tomatina festival involves throwing over 150,000 tomatoes in a giant food fight.

Europe.name: Sweden
Europe.funFact: Stockholm is built on 14 islands connected by 57 bridges.

Europe.name: Switzerland
Europe.funFact: Switzerland has four official languages: German, French, Italian, and Romansh.

Europe.name: Ukraine
Europe.funFact: Ukraine is the largest country entirely within Europe by area.

Europe.name: United Kingdom
Europe.funFact: The London Underground, opened in 1863, was the world's first underground railway.

Europe.name: Vatican City
Europe.funFact: Vatican City is the smallest country in the world by both area and population.

North America.name: Antigua and Barbuda
North America.funFact: Antigua has 365 beaches — one for every day of the year.

North America.name: Bahamas
North America.funFact: The Bahamas has the clearest ocean water in the world and one of the world's largest underwater cave systems.

North America.name: Barbados
North America.funFact: Barbados is the birthplace of rum, with the Mount Gay distillery dating back to 1703.

North America.name: Belize
North America.funFact: Belize has the second-largest barrier reef in the world and the Great Blue Hole.

North America.name: Canada
North America.funFact: Canada has the longest coastline of any country in the world at over 243,000 km.

North America.name: Costa Rica
North America.funFact: Costa Rica abolished its army in 1948 and redirected military spending to education and health.

North America.name: Cuba
North America.funFact: Cuba is the largest island in the Caribbean and is famous for its classic 1950s American cars.

North America.name: Dominica
North America.funFact: Dominica is known as the 'Nature Island of the Caribbean' and has the world's second-largest hot spring.

North America.name: Dominican Republic
North America.funFact: Santo Domingo was the first permanent European settlement in the Americas, founded in 1496.

North America.name: El Salvador
North America.funFact: El Salvador is the smallest and most densely populated country in Central America.

North America.name: Grenada
North America.funFact: Grenada is known as the 'Spice Island' because it produces about 20% of the world's nutmeg.

North America.name: Guatemala
North America.funFact: Guatemala's Tikal was one of the largest cities of the ancient Maya civilization.

North America.name: Haiti
North America.funFact: Haiti was the first independent nation of Latin America and the Caribbean, and the first Black republic.

North America.name: Honduras
North America.funFact: Honduras is home to the ancient Maya ruins of Copán, known for its elaborate sculptured hieroglyphic stairway.

North America.name: Jamaica
North America.funFact: Jamaica was the birthplace of reggae music and Bob Marley.

North America.name: Mexico
North America.funFact: Mexico City is built on the ruins of the Aztec capital Tenochtitlan and is sinking about 10 inches per year.

North America.name: Nicaragua
North America.funFact: Nicaragua is the largest country in Central America and has the largest lake in Central America, Lake Nicaragua.

North America.name: Panama
North America.funFact: The Panama Canal connects the Atlantic and Pacific Oceans and saves ships a 12,000-mile journey around South America.

North America.name: Saint Kitts and Nevis
North America.funFact: Saint Kitts and Nevis is the smallest sovereign state in the Americas by both area and population.

North America.name: Saint Lucia
North America.funFact: Saint Lucia's Pitons, twin volcanic spires, are a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

North America.name: Saint Vincent and the Grenadines
North America.funFact: Several Pirates of the Caribbean movies were filmed on the islands of Saint Vincent and the Grenadines.

North America.name: Trinidad and Tobago
North America.funFact: Trinidad and Tobago has the largest natural asphalt lake in the world, Pitch Lake.

North America.name: United States
North America.funFact: The United States has the world's largest economy and is the third-largest country by area.

South America.name: Argentina
South America.funFact: Argentina is home to the widest avenue in the world, Avenida 9 de Julio in Buenos Aires.

South America.name: Bolivia
South America.funFact: Bolivia's Salar de Uyuni is the world's largest salt flat, spanning over 10,000 square km.

South America.name: Brazil
South America.funFact: Brazil contains about 60% of the Amazon Rainforest, the largest tropical rainforest on Earth.

South America.name: Chile
South America.funFact: Chile is the longest north-south country in the world, stretching over 4,300 km.

South America.name: Colombia
South America.funFact: Colombia is the world's leading producer of emeralds and the third-largest coffee producer.

South America.name: Ecuador
South America.funFact: Ecuador is named after the Equator, which runs through the country, and owns the Galápagos Islands.

South America.name: Guyana
South America.funFact: Guyana is the only English-speaking country in South America.

South America.name: Paraguay
South America.funFact: Paraguay is one of two landlocked countries in South America and generates nearly 100% of its electricity from hydropower.

South America.name: Peru
South America.funFact: Machu Picchu in Peru is a 15th-century Inca citadel set high in the Andes Mountains.

South America.name: Suriname
South America.funFact: Suriname is the smallest country in South America and over 90% of it is covered by tropical rainforest.

South America.name: Uruguay
South America.funFact: Uruguay hosted and won the first FIFA World Cup in 1930.

South America.name: Venezuela
South America.funFact: Angel Falls in Venezuela is the world's highest uninterrupted waterfall at 979 metres.

Oceania.name: Australia
Oceania.funFact: Australia's Great Barrier Reef is the world's largest coral reef system and is visible from space.

Oceania.name: Fiji
Oceania.funFact: Fiji consists of over 330 islands, of which about 110 are permanently inhabited.

Oceania.name: Kiribati
Oceania.funFact: Kiribati is the only country in the world situated in all four hemispheres.

Oceania.name: Marshall Islands
Oceania.funFact: The Marshall Islands were the site of 67 nuclear tests by the United States between 1946 and 1958.

Oceania.name: Micronesia
Oceania.funFact: Micronesia uses giant stone discs called Rai stones as a form of traditional currency on Yap Island.

Oceania.name: Nauru
Oceania.funFact: Nauru is the smallest island nation in the world and the third-smallest country by area.

Oceania.name: New Zealand
Oceania.funFact: New Zealand was the first country to give women the right to vote in national elections in 1893.

Oceania.name: Palau
Oceania.funFact: Palau's Jellyfish Lake contains millions of golden jellyfish that have lost their ability to sting.

Oceania.name: Papua New Guinea
Oceania.funFact: Papua New Guinea has over 850 indigenous languages, more than any other country in the world.

Oceania.name: Samoa
Oceania.funFact: Samoa was the first country to see each new day due to its position near the International Date Line.

Oceania.name: Solomon Islands
Oceania.funFact: The Solomon Islands have some of the most biodiverse coral reefs in the world.

Oceania.name: Tonga
Oceania.funFact: Tonga is the only Pacific Island nation that was never colonized by a European power.

Oceania.name: Tuvalu
Oceania.funFact: Tuvalu earns millions of dollars from licensing its .tv internet domain name.

Oceania.name: Vanuatu
Oceania.funFact: Vanuatu has the world's most accessible active volcano, Mount Yasur on Tanna Island.
